mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate indicates a tight monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and dampens corporate investment and consumer spending, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS over a 5-year rolling window, or the correlation coefficient is positive.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals an overheated labor market, potentially leading to wage inflation and reduced corporate profit margins, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of observed instances over a 7-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1500k units
failure_condition: When both RSAFS growth and HOUST exceed their thresholds, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signifies increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which is a strong indicator of economic recovery and improved corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery periods, positive INDPRO growth does not lead to statistically significant positive SPX 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects a strong and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby supporting and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the longer term.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth over a 10-year rolling window, or the correlation coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often triggers tighter monetary policy, all of which are detrimental to corporate profitability and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Weak payroll employment growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, indicating an economic slowdown that will negatively impact consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth < 0.1% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth is weak and UNRATE is rising, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of cases over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in retail sales indicates a sharp decline in consumer spending, which directly translates to lower corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic contraction.
failure_condition: During economic contraction periods, negative RSAFS growth does not lead to statistically significant negative SPX 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.1).
